danger_signals.visual_form: Structures built from steel, stone, flesh, bone, and corruption; organic-mechanical hybrid aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Presence of Hell's invasion and dimensional breach; areas under demonic control or contamination

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, grotesque vessel with enormous gates; built from mixed organic and inorganic materials
danger_signals.meaning: Critical threat event—direct invasion from Hell; catastrophic danger imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Imps, Cyberdemons, Arch-viles, Spider Masterminds, and other Hell entities appearing in base areas
danger_signals.meaning: Active demonic presence; dimensional gateway breached; area is hostile

danger_signals.visual_form: Converted human soldiers as undead enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Fallen comrades; base security compromised; former allies now threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Damaged UAC facility, destroyed equipment, overrun structures
danger_signals.meaning: Complete base failure; loss of control; safe zones eliminated

safety_signals.visual_form: Structured, technological human facilities with clear architectural order
safety_signals.meaning: Human-controlled space; research/military infrastructure; relative safety from Hell

safety_signals.visual_form: Doom marine loadout: weapons, ammo, health packs, armor pickups
safety_signals.meaning: Player preparation and survival capability; resistance is possible

safety_signals.visual_form: Clean, functional base compartments before demonic invasion spreads
safety_signals.meaning: Temporarily secure areas; containment still holds in some sectors

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zombified soldiers (former UAC personnel converted to demonic servants)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Technological base architecture; moon facility infrastructure; research complex with Quantum Accelerator; organized military installations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Icon of Sin (final boss demon entity)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple demon types: Imps, Cyberdemons, Arch-viles, Spider Masterminds, generic demons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Organic-mechanical hybrid structures; flesh-and-bone architecture; corrupted materials; demonic spaceship; dimensional gateways; eerie sky textures (flame-filled or dark ambient)
Military equipment, weapons caches, ammo depots, human body armor; base control rooms

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Pre-invasion: Baseline security and research operations
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Functional UAC base with organized infrastructure; early experimental gateway setup; controlled environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lax radar operators; confident personnel; routine supply operations

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Gateway breach: First dimensional aperture opened
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Initial demonic entities (Imps) emerge; dimensional portal visible; base alert systems activate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Marines successfully repel initial incursion; confidence remains; security measures focused on gateway containment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Massive corrupted vessel descends; enormous demonic gates open; rain of demons floods the base; structures collapse and burn; architectural corruption spreads
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Complete base overrun; personnel slain or zombified; all defenses fail; facility becomes demon stronghold; marine commander separated from main force

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-invasion Earth: Global catastrophe aftermath (Plutonia context)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Secret research complex built in response; Quantum Accelerator device present; defensive infrastructure replaces offensive research; darker, more heavily fortified aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Global governments mobilize; UAC reformed under new management; focus shifts to invasion prevention rather than Hell exploration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ruined skull of Icon of Sin; blue light emanating from destroyed demon-spitter; sense of climax and uncertainty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Something continues to rumble in distance; victory is hollow; threat remains despite primary objective completion

environmental_storytelling.example: Transition from clean, organized UAC base corridors to corrupted organic-mechanical hybrid structures signals demonic infiltration and loss of human control
Hell spaceship built from 'steel, stone, flesh, bone, and corruption' communicates that Hell weaponizes and corrupts human materials and structures
Enormous demonic gates and massive spaceship establish Hell as an overwhelming external threat, not internal or containable
Levels feature flame-filled or dark starry skies replacing normal vistas, visually indicating dimensional breach and atmospheric corruption
Presence of high-tier demons (Cyberdemons, Arch-viles) in specific areas marks critical threat zones and boss encounter areas
TNT depicts reactive research base on Jupiter moon; Plutonia depicts defensive research complex on Earth—visual infrastructure reflects strategic shift from exploration to containment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Dismiss unusual radar reading of Hell spaceship as false alarm; fail to raise alarm despite visual anomalies
Flee to face 'terrible truth'; scattered and killed or zombified as demonic forces overflow base defenses
Swears vengeance; methodically pursues Icon of Sin; fights toward climactic confrontation despite overwhelming odds
Rain down continuously from Hell spaceship gates; overrun all defensive positions; convert defeated soldiers to undead servants
Serves as final objective; defeat triggers rumbling and blue light manifestation, suggesting incomplete resolution or lingering threat

notes: Final Doom consists of two distinct episodes with different narrative framings: TNT: Evilution presents an immediate invasion scenario on a UAC moon base, while The Plutonia Experiment frames Hell threat as a global strategic problem requiring technological solution (Quantum Accelerator). Visually, this translates to TNT's more chaotic, combat-focused aesthetic versus Plutonia's deliberate defense-focused complex design. The PlayStation version reduced difficulty and simplified layouts, but maintained core visual language of human vs. demonic architecture conflict. Sky textures and structural corruption serve as primary world-state communicators. No explicit faction colors or sigils are documented, but architectural style (ordered tech vs. organic corruption) and enemy type distributions clearly mark territorial control and threat levels.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Final Doom (gameplay, plot, development, world-state narrative)
